description: US-16 Maintain a Customer's Record — Household, address, group, notes and certificate renewal editing on the customer record, with counts, portions and price updating immediately.

userStories.title: updateHousehold use case
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to add and remove household members, so the record reflects reality as households change. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.1
acceptanceCriteria: updateHousehold(deps, {customerId, members}) replaces the household member set
An empty member list rejects with EmptyHousehold and a future birthdate with BirthDateInFuture, reusing the registration domain rules rather than a second implementation
Derived counts, portions and price are never written
Test asserts a change that alters the counts puts the customer on the cards-due list with reason HOUSEHOLD_CHANGE
Test asserts a change that does not alter the counts (e.g. a spelling fix) does not put the customer on that list
Test asserts no history of the previous composition is kept
Writes an audit entry
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: updateCustomerDetails and updateNotes use cases
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to correct a customer's personal data, address and notes. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.2 and §US-16.3
acceptanceCriteria: updateCustomerDetails updates name, date of birth and address with the same validation as registration

The customer's own record and their household-member entry stay consistent when their name or birthdate changes; the mechanism is decided, documented and tested (a single source of truth is preferred)
The customer number is not editable through this use case
updateNotes saves free text as given; empty is allowed, and a maximum length (4000 chars) is enforced with a clear message
Test asserts notes are returned by the counter lookup
Both write audit entries
Tested against hand-written fakes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: changeGroup use case
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want to move a customer between Red and Blue, so the two groups stay roughly balanced. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.4
acceptanceCriteria: changeGroup(deps, {customerId, group}) sets the group and returns the resulting group sizes
Changing to the same group rejects as a no-op
Test asserts a customer moved to Red is servable in a Red week on the same day
The change puts the customer on the cards-due list with reason GROUP_CHANGE, since the card prints the group
Writes an audit entry
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer record editing on /kunden/[id]
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want one screen where I can see and update everything about a customer. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.5
acceptanceCriteria: Household members can be added and removed inline; counts, portions and price update live
The group control shows both current group sizes next to the choice
Notes are a multi-line field saved explicitly, with an indication that they appear at the counter
The certificate renewal form is embedded and shows the reminder count resetting to 0
Destructive actions (reissue card, block/unblock, archive) are grouped in a clearly separated section, each with its own confirmation
Distribution history is listed newest first with date, showed-up, paid and the price that applied
Each household member shows their current age next to their birthdate
An archived customer's record renders fully read-only
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: edit a household and see the effects
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving record edits propagate to counts, price, group and the counter. Source: tasks/prd-us-16-maintain-customer-record.md §US-16.6
acceptanceCriteria: Spec opens a customer record, adds a child, and asserts counts, portions and price update and that the customer appears on the cards-due list
Spec changes the group and asserts both group sizes update and the counter verdict for today flips accordingly
Spec edits the notes and asserts the text appears on the counter screen for that customer
Spec records a renewed certificate and asserts the reminder count shows 0
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
